About & Contact

Learn more and get in touch

About the Project

I am a data visualization designer, developer, and public health analyst based in Minneapolis. I studied science communication through graphic design in London, UK, before diving into a master's in public health at the University of Minnesota.

I explore biology, human health, epidemiology, and evolution through data visualization. I primarily use R, ggplot2, Python, JavaScript's D3 library, and the Adobe Creative Suite, as well as a smattering of other tools to bring datasets to life. The world has never collected more data, and data visualization is a powerful tool for drawing insights out of spreadsheets and databases.

I also write science fiction and poetry in my free time to explore future possibilities and life on Earth.

← Back to Data Visualization

Background

Resilience Continuum is a project by Claire Nyquist which explores science through data visualization. I'm excited about the combination of the apparently disparate fields of design and public health as they come together in data visualization.
